summary: Full backend + frontend testing of JulianTees streetwear site. All 8 backend pytest tests passed (health, contact valid + 422 cases, newsletter valid + idempotent + 422). Frontend verified end-to-end: brand logo, hero headline 'WEAR WHAT YOU mean.', 6 category cards, lookbook section, no TikTok in footer, JSON-LD ClothingStore + SEO meta tags, all 5 nav links, vintage filter activates on category click, 11 product cards with target=_blank, newsletter + contact form success toasts, mobile menu drawer opens with all nav links.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 107 raises HTTPException 502 on Resend failure even though message is already stored — consider returning 200 with emailed=false instead so user UX isn't penalized for an email-provider outage.
Empty contact form submission relies on HTML5 native required validation (no toast). Consider a custom JS-level error toast for consistency with success toast UX.
CORS allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec; tighten origins for production.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Newsletter test_idem_* and contact TEST_* records created via API tests. Idempotency verified at DB level (1 record per email).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All requested features pass. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py covers all endpoints. Frontend data-testids exist for: brand-logo, hero-title, category-card-{graphic|vintage|travel|streetwear|minimal|trendy}, filter-{all|graphic|vintage|...}, lookbook-section, newsletter-{section|title|form|input|submit}, contact-{section|name|email|subject|message|submit|form|email-link}, mobile-menu-toggle, nav-contact. Hero headline visible text is single-line 'WEAR WHAT YOU mean.' (visually wraps via CSS).
